title: The Burgomaster's Funeral & the Church
subtitle: The Village of Barovia · The Cemetery · The Church of the Morning Lord
opener: Ireena Kolyana was not what the letter had implied. The burgomaster's plea spoke of someone to be protected. The woman who met them at the mansion door was already protecting herself — composed, direct, and completely aware of the situation she was in. She accepted the group's help because she was not foolish enough to refuse it, not because she needed rescuing.

paragraphs: Ismark's request was straightforward: bury their father before the party left, then escort Ireena to Vallaki. The group discussed it. Not everyone was immediately certain. But the answer was yes.

sections.heading: The Church — Before the Funeral
paragraphs: They entered the church of the Morning Lord before the burial service. Something was audible from below — a sound from the basement, something between an animal and a human voice, intermittent and wrong. They asked Father Donavich about it. He looked at them the way a man looks at something he has decided not to see. He said nothing and returned to his preparations.

sections.heading: The Burial
paragraphs: The cemetery was quiet in the way Barovia is always quiet — not peaceful, just empty of resistance. Father Donavich performed the rites with mechanical precision. He was present in body. Whatever animated him as a priest, as a man, was somewhere else entirely. The village sent almost no one.

sections.heading: Donavich Speaks
paragraphs: After the earth covered Kolyan Indirovich, the father finally talked. His son Doru had followed the monster hunter Rudolph van Richten to Castle Ravenloft, believing they could end Strahd. Doru came back changed — a vampire spawn, barely recognisable, chained in the church basement where his father had been keeping him for months, praying for a miracle that had not come.

Donavich gave the group something useful alongside the grief: a clear account of vampire weaknesses, the tools that could hurt them, the conditions that could kill them. And one more thing — if Strahd were destroyed, Doru would be freed from his control. The spawn do not outlast their master.
The group looked at each other. Killing Strahd was already the implicit objective. Now it had a secondary reward attached to it.
They left Doru chained in the basement. There was nothing else to do.

sections.heading: Sarda at the Church
paragraphs: Sarda felt it most — a father who could not release his son, a son who could not return. Faith as a structure assumes the structure holds. In Barovia, the Morning Lord's church had been standing for a long time, but the Morning Lord had been very quiet.

sections.heading: The Village Shop
paragraphs: Before returning to Ismark's, the group visited the village's only shop. The proprietor was a study in cheerful dishonesty — always smiling, aggressively optimistic about the quality of goods that did not merit optimism, and pricing things with the confidence of a man who knows he is the only option for fifty miles. They bought what they needed, paid too much, and left with the faint sense of having been robbed by someone who genuinely enjoyed it.
Back at the mansion, Ireena had a bag ready. Kolyan was in the ground. Vallaki was on the map. The route existed. Strahd knew where they were.
The road would not be quiet.

known: Doru followed Van Richten to Ravenloft and returned as a vampire spawn
If Strahd dies, Doru is freed from his control
Vampire weaknesses: sunlight, running water, holy symbols, stakes
Ireena is ready and willing to leave for Vallaki
The village shop owner will rob you with a smile
unanswered: Why is Strahd fixated on Ireena above all others?
Where is Rudolph van Richten now?
What awaits on the road to Vallaki?
people: Ireena Kolyana — composed, capable, Strahd's target
Doru — chained in the church basement, left there
